e_visa.nationality: Non-African nationals subject to reciprocity principle
e_visa.notes: On 1 August 2025, Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko announced a mandatory e-visa (advance online prepayment required) for nationals of countries outside Africa that impose visa requirements on Senegalese citizens. Likely affected: France, USA, Canada, UK, China. The system was officially announced but the portal (visasenegal.sn) was not operational as of June 2026. No official fee per applicant nor implementation date had been published. ECOWAS citizens remain exempt. The policy has not changed the on-the-ground entry regime as of the validation date.

visa_policy.notes: Two operative tiers apply as of June 2026: Category A = fully visa-exempt for ordinary passport holders (ECOWAS members, most EU/EEA states, and bilateral-agreement countries); Category B = free visa on arrival at Dakar Blaise Diagne International Airport (DSS) only, up to 90 days. Category C = visa required before arrival. A reciprocity-based e-visa for non-African nationals was announced August 2025 but remains non-operational. ECOWAS countries (including Cote d'Ivoire, Ghana, Sierra Leone) are all Category A. Several countries have split treatment: ordinary passport = Category B, diplomatic/service = Category A.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Algerian diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Austrian diplomatic passports have additional bilateral waiver. Ordinary passport holders are already Category A (visa-exempt).
Holders of Brazilian diplomatic and service passports are exempt from visa under bilateral agreement; ordinary passport holders require visa prior to entry (Category C).

Holders of Chinese diplomatic, service, and official (passports for public affairs) passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B). Agreement entered into force 2014-05-03.
Holders of Gabonese diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Iranian diplomatic passports are exempt from visa for a stay of one month; ordinary passport holders require visa prior to entry (Category C).
Holders of Kenyan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders are also Category A per embassy listing.
Holders of Libyan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).

Holders of Pakistani diplomatic, official, and service passports are exempt from visa under the Agreement on Visa Abolition for Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service Passports signed between Pakistan and Senegal (approximately 2021; exact date not confirmed on official source).
Holders of South African diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders are also Category A per embassy listing.
Holders of Tanzanian diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Turkish diplomatic passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Ugandan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment programme found for Senegal. The investment promotion agency APIX (investinsenegal.sn) promotes foreign direct investment but does not offer a CBI or second-passport scheme.

validation_notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: ~30 countries that the Embassy of Senegal (London) lists as Category A (visa-free for ordinary passports) were previously misclassified as Category B (visa on arrival). These include: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, India, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Kenya, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, South Korea, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, UAE, UK, USA, Vanuatu, Vatican, Zambia, Zimbabwe.

ADDED: Cote d'Ivoire (CIV), Ghana (GHA), Sierra Leone (SLE) — ECOWAS members confirmed as Category A visa-free by official embassy listing. These were missing from the prior record despite being noted as gaps.

ADDED to visa_on_arrival: New Zealand (NZL), Philippines (PHL), Equatorial Guinea (GNQ), Libya ordinary (LBY), Palestinian Authority (PSE), Papua New Guinea (PNG), San Marino (SMR), Tanzania (TZA), China ordinary (CHN) — present on embassy page but absent from prior record.

REMOVED duplicate entries: Bulgaria, Canada, Mauritius, and several others were listed in BOTH visa_free and visa_on_arrival in the prior record; corrected to single correct classification (Category A = visa_free).

REMOVED Saint Martin (null iso3) — not a sovereign state; likely referring to French territory (covered under France) or Dutch territory (covered under Netherlands), both Category A; not separately listed on official embassy page.
CORRECTED: France, UK, USA, Canada, India, Japan, Singapore, UAE, South Korea — all moved from visa_on_arrival to visa_free (Category A) per official embassy listing.

E-visa entry updated: clarified that the announced mandatory e-visa (August 2025, PM Sonko) remains non-operational as of June 2026; portal visasenegal.sn is non-functional per EntryCheck; existing Category A/B regime remains in effect.

REMOVED conditional_access entries for Belgium (now correctly Category A ordinary), France diplomatic (France ordinary is Category A, diplomatic also Category A — no split needed), Netherlands (ordinary is Category A, diplomatic also waived — no meaningful split), Portugal, Romania, Spain (all ordinary = Category A, diplomatic waivers redundant), since ordinary passport already gets Category A access.
ADDED conditional_access entries for Gabon, South Africa, Tanzania — split treatment confirmed (diplomatic/service = Category A, ordinary = Category B).
diplomatie.gouv.sn SSL certificate expired — site unreachable; ambsencanada.org removed from official_domains as it is not a .gouv.sn domain.
Suriname iso3 corrected: SUR is the ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for Suriname (not SUR which was used — SUR = Suriname, correct).
gaps: The official e-visa portal (visasenegal.sn) announced August 2025 was non-functional as of June 2026; no official per-applicant fee, affected country list, or launch date has been published on a .gouv.sn domain.
diplomatie.gouv.sn SSL certificate is expired; the Ministry of Foreign Affairs website cannot be verified for the current visa policy text.

Full Category B country list could not be exhaustively verified directly from a .sn government domain; the Embassy of Senegal (London) page (senegal-embassy.uk) was used as the primary official source, which is an embassy site.
Pakistan-Senegal diplomatic/service passport visa abolition agreement exact date not confirmed from official text; only Pakistan MFA press release available.
The complete Category C (visa required) country list was not individually enumerated in this record — only visa-free and visa-on-arrival entries are listed; Category C is the residual default.
No official RBI (residence by investment / golden visa) programme was found or confirmed.
No official fast-track / digital nomad / startup visa programme was found.
Russia and Serbia diplomatic/service passport waivers reported on Wikipedia but not confirmed from the official embassy page or EITI PDF; not included in conditional_access.
Djibouti inclusion in Category A is confirmed by the embassy listing but exact max_stay_days not stated separately — 90 days applied by analogy with other Category A entries.

outbound_official.note: no official Senegal government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Senegal's Ministry of African Integration and Foreign Affairs (diplomatie.gouv.sn) publishes only inbound visa rules (who may enter Senegal); no page listing destinations accessible to Senegalese ordinary-passport holders was found on any .gouv.sn domain. The SSL certificate on diplomatie.gouv.sn is expired as of June 2026, further preventing verification. Consular sites (consulsen-paris.gouv.sn, dgse.gouv.sn) cover only document issuance (passport renewal, civil records) for Senegalese nationals abroad — not outbound visa-free destination lists.
